key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn the static mechanical response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics from microstructural topology and history, replacing expensive mesoscale FE simulations.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid, physics-informed screening of weak microstructural regions in SFT parts to support design optimization, digital twins, and damage-aware materials engineering.
method: Reconstructed microstructures from micro-CT and discretized them into Voronoi-based interaction cells.
Ran nonlinear finite element homogenization for each cell, including matrix damage, to generate stress-strain training data.
Trained a hybrid Graph Neural Network-Long Short-Term Memory model to encode spatial connectivity and evolving mechanical response.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matched unseen FE responses with about R2  0.98 while reducing computational cost by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the reconstructed microstructures, so extrapolation to new fiber architectures or processing conditions may require recalibration.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
